transcript: But when he arrives on site, he finds five epistatai, or city officials, waiting to confront him. They accuse Phidias of embezzling gold designated for the temple's sacred central statue. He has until sundown to provide all the temple's expenses and account for every flake of gold, or face the judgment of the courts. Though he's insulted by these false charges, Phidias isn't surprised. Pericles, the prince of Persia,
